evidence.scientific_clarity: Spring-layout force-directed graph with 4 community colors, tuned edge alpha=0.25 and node_size=220 cleanly shows community structure as briefed.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype set; default matplotlib font (DejaVu Sans) used throughout — text will be bitmapped in PDF and font is the disallowed default.
evidence.palette: Custom 4-color categorical palette (#E63946, #2A9D8F, #F4A261, #457B9D) avoids red-green pairing but red/orange pair risks confusion for protanopes; not a certified CB-safe set.
evidence.layout_density: 7×7 in figure, k=1.8/sqrt(n) spacing, tight_layout, no overlapping labels expected at 30 nodes — well-proportioned use of space.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend placed lower-right, title present, caption matches figure; however caption lacks a quantitative finding (e.g. modularity score or edge count) that Reviewer 2 would request.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is saved but script omits matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42, so text is stored as Type 3 (bitmapped) rather than selectable TrueType glyphs.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial; exceeds minimum requirements.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names algorithm, seed, node count, and community count, and describes topology; missing a concrete quantitative claim (e.g. inter-community edge fraction or modularity value).

overall_strengths: All required output files present plus bonus SVG; visualization parameters (edge alpha, node size, spring-layout k) are well-tuned to avoid hairball for a 30-node graph.
Community structure is immediately legible: distinct hues, white node borders, and a clear legend with title make the categorical encoding unambiguous.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype=0) means PDF/SVG text is not selectable or editable — a hard requirement for journal submission.
Default DejaVu Sans font and no explicit rcParams font family; caption lacks a numerical finding, which will draw a Reviewer 2 comment asking for quantitative support.
